SEARCH
Package org.apache.arrow.datafusion.logical_expr

Interface ScalarUDF

All Known Implementing Classes:
SimpleScalarUDF
public interface ScalarUDF
A user-defined scalar function that can be registered with a SessionContext.

Implement this interface to create custom scalar functions usable in SQL queries. For simple fixed-type UDFs, use Functions.createUdf(java.lang.String, org.apache.arrow.datafusion.logical_expr.Volatility, java.util.List<org.apache.arrow.vector.types.pojo.ArrowType>, org.apache.arrow.vector.types.pojo.ArrowType, org.apache.arrow.datafusion.logical_expr.ScalarUDFImpl) instead.

Example usage: 

import static org.apache.arrow.datafusion.Functions.*;

ScalarUDF pow = createUdf("pow", Volatility.IMMUTABLE,
    List.of(ArrowType.FloatingPoint(FloatingPointPrecision.DOUBLE),
            ArrowType.FloatingPoint(FloatingPointPrecision.DOUBLE)),
    ArrowType.FloatingPoint(FloatingPointPrecision.DOUBLE),
    (args, numRows, allocator) -> {
        Float8Vector base = (Float8Vector) args.get(0);
        Float8Vector exp = (Float8Vector) args.get(1);
        Float8Vector result = new Float8Vector("result", allocator);
        result.allocateNew(numRows);
        for (int i = 0; i < numRows; i++) {
            result.set(i, Math.pow(base.get(i), exp.get(i)));
        }
        result.setValueCount(numRows);
        return result;
    });
ctx.registerUdf(pow, allocator);
See Also:
  • Rust DataFusion: ScalarUDF

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    default List<org.apache.arrow.vector.types.pojo.Field>
    coerceTypes(List<org.apache.arrow.vector.types.pojo.Field> argFields)
    Performs type coercion on the input argument fields.
    org.apache.arrow.vector.FieldVector

    Returns the name of this function as it will appear in SQL.

  • Method Details

    • name

      String name()
      Returns the name of this function as it will appear in SQL.

      Example: 

      @Override
public String name() {
    return "my_function";
}
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: ScalarUDF::name

    • signature

      Signature signature()
      Returns the signature of this function, including its volatility.

      Example: 

      @Override
public Signature signature() {
    return new Signature(Volatility.IMMUTABLE);
}
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: ScalarUDF::signature

    • returnField

      org.apache.arrow.vector.types.pojo.Field returnField(List<org.apache.arrow.vector.types.pojo.Field> argFields)
      Determines the return field (name + type) given the argument fields.

      Example: 

      @Override
public Field returnField(List<Field> argFields) {
    return Field.nullable("result", new ArrowType.Int(64, true));
}
      Parameters:
      argFields - the fields of the input arguments
      Returns:
      the field describing the return type
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: ScalarUDF::return_field_from_args

    • invoke

      org.apache.arrow.vector.FieldVector invoke(List<org.apache.arrow.vector.FieldVector> args, List<org.apache.arrow.vector.types.pojo.Field> argFields, int numRows, org.apache.arrow.vector.types.pojo.Field returnField, org.apache.arrow.memory.BufferAllocator allocator)
      Invokes the function on the given input vectors.

      Example: 

      @Override
public FieldVector invoke(List<FieldVector> args, List<Field> argFields,
        int numRows, Field returnField, BufferAllocator allocator) {
    BigIntVector input = (BigIntVector) args.get(0);
    BigIntVector result = new BigIntVector("result", allocator);
    result.allocateNew(numRows);
    for (int i = 0; i < numRows; i++) {
        result.set(i, input.get(i) * 2);
    }
    result.setValueCount(numRows);
    return result;
}
      Parameters:
      args - the input vectors
      argFields - the fields describing each input argument
      numRows - the number of rows to process
      returnField - the expected return field
      allocator - the buffer allocator for creating the result vector
      Returns:
      the result vector
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: ScalarUDF::invoke_with_args

    • coerceTypes

      default List<org.apache.arrow.vector.types.pojo.Field> coerceTypes(List<org.apache.arrow.vector.types.pojo.Field> argFields)
      Performs type coercion on the input argument fields. The default implementation returns the argument fields unchanged.

      Example: 

      @Override
public List<Field> coerceTypes(List<Field> argFields) {
    return List.of(Field.nullable("arg0", new ArrowType.Int(64, true)));
}
      Parameters:
      argFields - the fields of the input arguments
      Returns:
      the coerced fields
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: ScalarUDF::coerce_types
